{{Quote|Holt Kazed! Holt Kazed!|[[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]] to Nelvaanians about [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker's]] mechanical hand|<ref name="CW23" />}}
'''Orvos''' was the elderly [[Shaman/Legends|shaman]] of the [[City/Legends|village]] of [[Rokrul]] on the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] of [[Nelvaan/Legends|Nelvaan]] during the last [[Standard year/Legends|years]] of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]. Near the end of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] between the Republic and the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]], Orvos assisted the [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]] and the [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]] by informing them metaphorically of the [[Techno Union/Legends|Techno Union]] presence that was decimating the planet. The shaman then prophesied that Skywalker was the [[Holt Kazed]], or "Ghost Hand," based on the fact that Skywalker had a cybernetic [[Limb/Legends|arm]], and that he would save the Nelvaanian people. He then sent Skywalker on a solo mission to bring back the [[Gender/Legends|male]] villagers that had been captured by the Techno Union.

==Biography==
Orvos was a [[Nelvaanian/Legends|Nelvaanian]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] native to the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Nelvaan/Legends|Nelvaan]] in the [[Koobi system]] of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]]. He was [[Shaman/Legends|shaman]] of the [[City/Legends|village]] of [[Rokrul]] during the [[The galaxy/Legends|galaxy]]-spanning [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] between the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] and the rebelling [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]]. Some [[Standard year/Legends|years]] prior to [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]], the last year of the Clone Wars, Orvos predicted an ice age would fall over Nelvaan. His prediction came true when the Confederacy-aligned [[Techno Union/Legends|Techno Union]] set up a [[Techno Union laboratory|laboratory]] that ran off a [[siphon generator]]. The generator sucked the thermal energy from the surrounding area, making temperatures plummet across the world. However, despite Orvos prophecy, the fierceness and obstinacy of the winter shocked the villagers.<ref name="DB1" /> The Nelvaanians sent out their male warriors and scouts to discover the reason behind the cold, but none of them came back. Even Orvos was without explanation as to the cause of their disappearance. However, Orvos did foresee two men coming to Nelvaan and discovering the cause of their plight.<ref name="DB2">{{DB|location|nelvaan|Nelvaan}}</ref>

In the year 19 BBY, [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]] and his [[Jedi Master/Legends|Master]] [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]] landed on the planet in search of the Separatist [[General/Legends|General]] [[Grievous/Legends|Grievous]]. After crossing paths with a ferocious [[Horax/Legends|horax]] and disrupting the coming-of-age ritual of Rokrul's chieftain's [[Tuzes-Adaz's son|son]], the chieftain, whose name was [[Tuzes-Adaz]], brought the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] to the village to meet Orvos. Later that night, Orvos, the Jedi, and the villagers gathered around a campfire as the shaman attempted to contact the [[Spirit of the Fire]]. After receiving a sign from the smoke, Orvos determined that Skywalker was the prophesied [[Holt Kazed]], or "Ghost Hand," the one would free the villagers from their wintery misery.<ref name="CW23" />

Orvos then prepared Skywalker for his journey through the wilderness by using [[bruise-leech crawler]]s to create a rectangular tattoo design over his chest. He explained to Skywalker how to heal the "[[Great Mother]]," the Nelvaanian term for their planet, by telling him to follow "the path of her frozen tears to where her inner flame had languished."<ref name="CW23" /> Orvos regretfully did not tell the Jedi the entire story, leaving out the part where the "Ghost Hand" would eventually, over time, take over Skywalker's body and consume him, block out the light, and strangle all his loved ones, for he felt the Great Mother needed Skywalker's strength no matter the consequences.<ref name="DB1" /> Skywalker would go on to find the Nelvaanian men (ensnared by General Grievous for mutation by the Separatist war machine<ref name="Blog 55">{{Blog|abelgpena|55|Endnotes for The Story of General Grievous, Part 2: The Knight Slayer}}</ref>), [[Battle of Nelvaan|destroy]] the Techno Union lab, and return the men to their village. There the shaman and the villagers had waited for his return, and after some initial hesitation, the tribe's warriors were overjoyed to be reunited with the others.<ref name="CW25">{{CW|25}}</ref>

==Personality and traits==
Orvos was a very spiritual Nelvaanian. He had strong faith in the deities of the Nelvaanian people, such as the Spirit of the Fire and the Great Mother, and was very concerned for the latter's well-being.<ref name="CW23" /> The shaman was also able to make accurate prophesies regarding future events, having made at least two that came to pass during the Clone Wars.<ref name="CW25" />

==Behind the scenes==
The article on Tuzes-Adaz in ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' alternatively calls the shaman "Orvos" and "Ovros", the second spelling having not been seen in any other source.<ref name="CSWE2">{{CSWECite|III|269|Tuzes-Adaz}}</ref>
